summary: Iteration 2 QA covered the three newly-shipped features: video upload in Feed, friend-request flow + inbox, and the Discovery grid redesign. 16/16 new pytest cases pass plus 39/40 regression (1 conditional skip carried over from iteration 1). End-to-end UI flows (signup -> Discovery grid -> Like/Friend -> Likes page sections -> Feed video compose & playback) all succeeded. No critical bugs found.

issues: Author name still falls back to 'Player' for older API-created accounts (carried over from iteration 1 — not introduced by this change).

action_items: (Optional) Cleanup TEST_-prefixed seed Firebase users + posts created during QA in production Mongo.
(Carry-over) Consider fixing author fallback name from 'Player' to email prefix or 'Anonymous'.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 805 lines — past the 700 line guideline. Splitting friend-requests, posts, and likes into routers/ modules is overdue.

send_friend_request: when reverse_req exists the code does 3 Mongo writes (insert match, addToSet both, delete request). Could be collapsed to 2 writes (single update with $setOnInsert + $addToSet) for atomicity.
accept_friend_request also issues 3 separate updates on the matches doc; consider combining $setOnInsert + $addToSet in a single update_one call.

posts.py sniff_video_mime treats any file with bytes[4:8]=='ftyp' as mp4 (default branch). Fragmented .3gp / .heic files share this header — recommend explicit brand whitelist (isom/mp41/mp42/avc1/qt) and reject others.

create_post_endpoint reads the entire upload into memory before validating size (the 25MB cap is checked after .read()). For large uploads this still allocates 25MB+ in RAM per request — fine for short videos but worth noting under load.
/api/inbox does N+1 user lookups (one find_one per friend-request and per incoming like). At low scale this is fine; at growth, switch to a $lookup or batched find({'uid': {'$in': [...]}}).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (Discovery grid, Like/Friend, Likes sections, Feed video upload all verified)

test_credentials: Used Firebase Identity Toolkit REST API to mint ephemeral TEST_ users (cleaned up via fixture teardown). UI signup used qa3_<random>@example.com / demo1234.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created ~5 new test posts (mp4/webm) + 3 ephemeral Firebase Auth users (deleted on teardown). UI test created qa3_631790643e@example.com (NOT auto-deleted — recommend cleanup).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend coverage: /app/backend/tests/test_api.py (40 tests, legacy regression) + /app/backend/tests/test_new_features.py (16 tests, iteration-2 features). Re-run with: cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/ -v. UI flow exercised in this run: signup -> redirect to /feed (note: previous expectation of /discovery redirect is wrong; the app lands on /feed after auth/sync, which is correct). Discovery grid is 40 cards at /discovery, with both Like and Friend buttons per card and no Pass button. Likes page has 3 sections including the new friend-requests-section and incoming-likes-section, populated correctly by seed-sarah/seed-amanda auto-likes. Feed video upload pipeline (mp4/webm magic-byte sniff -> /api/uploads/<file>.mp4 -> <video> element render) works end-to-end.
rca of the issue: No regressions. Iteration-1's suspected LikesPage UI race is now superseded: after Like/Friend in Discovery, the user lands on /likes and the seeded auto-liked users correctly appear in the new incoming-likes section. Inbox de-duplication logic verified: a user who has both a pending FR and a like swipe is shown ONLY under friend-requests-section, not duplicated in incoming-likes-section.
